name: Clyde Hart
abstract: Clyde Hart (1910 – March 19, 1945) was an American jazz pianist and arranger. He was an important figure in the transition from swing to bebop.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: It was Prez, Clyde Hart, Doc West, and Shad Collins. It was a good band. ... I got him to come by our rehearsal, and just made Prez use him. And Prez loved him so we started using piano at this time.

about_quotes.speaker: Tiny Grimes
about_quotes.evidence: Clyde Hart was your regular piano player, right. I think Jimmy Butts, bass player. Clyde was off and on. Clyde was there -- low key -- quiet like me... Also Clyde Hart at one time or another.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Bourgeois
about_quotes.evidence: The band we used to call, Sharky called the band the Sharks of Rhythm while we were in New York, see? Santo and Irving Fazola, Clyde Hart, this is the record he made in New York.

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: late '44, I played several weeks, maybe two months with Don Byas, our trio, with Clyde Hart on piano

about_quotes.speaker: David Albert Francis
about_quotes.evidence: we got Clyde Hart in the band -- Clyde Hart was playing piano with us and arranging.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Now they all stayed on the bandstand... Ben Tharp... playing drums and myself.
